dialogueChunks.intro: He finished it. The blade is... honestly, it's the most beautiful thing I've ever seen. It hums when you hold it. Three years of his life, forged into steel.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The strange thing is what happened after. He set it on the anvil, stared at it for a long time, and then walked out of the forge. Went straight to the house where his wife has been staying. {target_npc} says they talked for hours. Whether the marriage survives, nobody knows yet. But for the first time in three years, the furnace is cold.
dialogueChunks.outro: You gave him the means to finish, {player_name}. Whether that was mercy or enabling, I suppose time will tell. {quest_ally} says the blade is worth a fortune. He hasn't tried to sell it. Says it's not about the blade anymore.

playerResponses.text: Sometimes you have to reach the end of something to find your way back.
I hope the blade was worth everything it cost.

dialogueChunks.intro: The alchemist succeeded. {quest_focus} works. It does exactly what she said it would. And she has absolutely no idea what to do next.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: She sat in the wreckage of her tower, holding the vial, and said: 'Now what?' Fifteen years of work, every relationship sacrificed, her health ruined, and the discovery is real. {target_npc} asked her if it was worth it. She said: 'Ask me in ten years, when I know whether anyone I loved is still willing to speak to me.'
dialogueChunks.outro: The discovery will change lives, {player_name}. Just not hers. Not yet. {quest_ally} is helping her publish the findings. Maybe recognition will fill some of the emptiness. Maybe nothing will.

playerResponses.text: Genius and loneliness seem to travel together. I hope she finds her way back.
The world will benefit even if she doesn't.
She chose the work over the people. That's nobody's fault but hers.

dialogueChunks.intro: The keystone fit. The collection is complete. And the collector sat on the floor of his vault and wept for twenty minutes.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Not from joy. From looking around the empty room and realizing what surrounded him: relics and dust instead of family. His daughter came back when she heard, bringing the grandchild again. This time, he held the baby. {target_npc} says he hasn't touched the collection since. He just sits in the garden with the child, pointing at birds.
dialogueChunks.outro: I don't know if the collection was worth the cost, {player_name}. But I know that finding the last piece broke the spell. He can see what he lost now. That's either the beginning of recovery or the beginning of grief. Maybe they're the same thing.

playerResponses.text: He's holding his grandchild instead of an artifact. That's a start.
Years of damage don't undo overnight. But at least he's trying.
